words.hint: More than friends but less than dating — nobody knows what it is
When you're texting someone constantly but haven't made it official
Someone you were talking to just stops responding completely
Someone gives you just enough attention to keep you interested but never fully commits
A sudden feeling of repulsion for someone you were attracted to
A behavior that signals this person might not be good for you
A positive sign that someone is emotionally healthy and worth your time
Natural charisma that makes someone irresistible — you either have it or you don't
When you post a photo hinting you're seeing someone without showing their face
When you post a photo of your partner with their face visible — making it official publicly
Overwhelming someone with affection and attention to gain control
Define The Relationship — the talk about what you two actually are
A relationship that builds slowly over time with lots of tension before anything happens
Acting like your life is a movie and you're the lead
Someone who does way too much for a person who doesn't like them back
When someone only sees you as a friend no matter what you do
Being the extra person with a couple who are clearly more into each other
A friend who helps you talk to your crush and hypes you up
When you realize you like someone more than you intended to
A former partner who keeps causing drama or showing up at the wrong times
Pretending to be less interested than you actually are to seem more desirable
They saw your message and deliberately didn't respond
Sending a second message before the person responded to your first
Things are progressing at a pace that makes someone uncomfortable
Feeling insecure because someone is spending time with another person
Difficulty believing someone is being honest due to past experiences
A relationship where you and your partner live far apart
Ending a relationship via message instead of in person
Dating someone new immediately after a breakup to get over the last person
The conversation or moment that helps you accept a relationship is over
Sending a direct message to someone you're interested in
A couple that keeps breaking up and getting back together repeatedly
The nervous excitement you feel when you like someone and see them
When someone makes you question your own memory or feelings
The physical and emotional pain of a relationship ending
The milestone where your partner officially meets your family
Not defining the relationship and going with the flow
The moment you two agree you're in a real relationship
When a close friendship ends — sometimes more painful than romantic breakups
Staying true to someone even when no one is watching
Friends who hook up but aren't in a relationship
When two people drift apart or have a major disagreement that ends the friendship
Being delusional about your chances with someone who doesn't like you back
Believing you can make someone like you back through positive thinking
The yearly celebration of when you started dating
Someone who puts themselves down or throws others under the bus to get attention from a specific person
When someone ghosts you but keeps liking your posts to stay in your periphery
When everyone wants to be in a relationship as the weather gets colder
Acting like you're destined for a perfect love story no matter what
An excuse to spend time together that could be academic or romantic
Asking someone to marry you, usually with a ring
Gen Z phrase meaning you're romantically texting someone but nothing is official
